metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'blocking-FP proxy' — give the formula (e.g. fraction of block-decision findings that resolve on the author's next edit, measured over a trailing N reviews), the computation window, and the script/sidecar field that computes it, co-located with the 10% demotion rule in the R7 child's AC.
evidence: Plan text: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
ABSENCE: no symbol, script, sidecar field, or docs page defines 'blocking-FP proxy' anywhere in the repo (searched src/, docs/, scripts/)

Existing tooling: scripts/plan_review_contamination_rate.py measures chunk contamination_rate, not FP rate; calibration uses rev_rr/validity/p90 per docs/experiments/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md
sidecar.py build_payload() persists decision/validity/priority/blocking_enabled fields but no 'blocking_fp_proxy' field
findings.finding: The Success Criteria specifies an operational demotion rule — 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory' — but 'blocking-FP proxy' is never defined anywhere in the plan, codebase, documentation, or experiment harnesses. No formula, no computation window, no script computes this metric. The existing tooling measures contamination_rate (chunk co-residence), and calibration replays report rev_rr/validity/p90 — none of which is called 'blocking-FP proxy'. The R7 dogfood-monitoring story cannot be implemented without knowing what to measure.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'new criteria enter at advisory POSTURE first...with R7 dogfood monitoring: when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory'
scenarios: The R7 child story's implementer cannot instrument the demotion trigger without a formula — any implementation will be a guess about what 'blocking-FP proxy' means.
A new criterion entering at advisory posture (per operator direction) can never trigger the defined demotion if the metric that should trigger it is undefined — the safety valve is inert.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to R7's AC: 'blocking-FP proxy = (number of block findings dropped by the author's immediate next re-review) / (total block findings in the review), measured as a trailing 20-review moving average per criterion, computed by scripts/plan_review_blocking_fp_rate.py reading REVIEW_RESULT sidecars.' Then add that field to sidecar.build_payload or a companion analysis script.

evidence: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/: 5342-568c-b881-4b2a & a880-b7e1-dc3e-407c (no ordering edge)
docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/: 5342-568c-b881-4b2a & b1a4-af7a-4a48-465d (no ordering edge)

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split SC2 into (a) an in-session close condition ('ships with no blocking-FP regression, validated by eval results') and (b) a standing post-ship operational rule ('when trailing FP proxy exceeds 10%, demote posture via criteria_routing.json change'), making clear only (a) is required to close this epic.
evidence: "trailing blocking-FP proxy" — 'trailing' implies an ongoing post-ship metric window, not an in-session check
The demotion rule and the ship-gate are expressed in the same success criterion without distinguishing which is a close condition vs. a post-ship governance rule
findings.finding: Success Criterion 2 conflates an in-session ship-gate ('no blocking-FP regression') with a post-deployment operational rule ('when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted'). The trailing metric requires multi-session production data to evaluate, so the boundary of when SC2 is fully satisfied is unclear — an executor may not know whether the demotion-trigger clause must also be verified before the epic closes.
findings.location: Success Criteria — SC2: 'The gate ships with no blocking-FP regression... when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory'
scenarios: Executor ships the implementation, verifies no FP regression in evals, and closes the epic; a reviewer reads SC2 as also requiring evidence that the demotion mechanism has been exercised (requires production data)
The epic remains open indefinitely waiting for a trailing-FP event that may not occur during the ship window

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile R4's gate numbers with E2's pre-registered thresholds: either (a) E2's README explicitly records R4's >=8/10 / <=1/10 as the class-4 pre-registered threshold, or (b) R4's AC explicitly defers to E2's pre-registered value.
evidence: E2 AC: 'Thresholds are pre-registered in the README in a commit that precedes any detector run against the corpus'
E2 What: 'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table'
R4 AC: '>=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants detected AND <=1/10 controls flagged'
Epic Context: 'child ACs are the authoritative gate rows; this epic does not duplicate the numbers'
findings.finding: R4's retrospective gate numbers (>=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants detected, <=1/10 controls flagged) live only in R4's AC and are not explicitly pre-registered in E2's pre-registration commit, creating a reconciliation gap: E2's README must pre-register thresholds for all 5 defect classes before any detector runs, but class (4)'s threshold is authored in R4 not E2 — if they diverge, the authoritative source is ambiguous.
findings.location: child 03a9-e6d1-4acc-435d (R4) and child d125-b4ce-c57f-4f06 (E2)
scenarios: E2's pre-registration README records a different threshold for class (4) than R4's AC (e.g., >=7/10 vs >=8/10). When R4's retrospective runs against E2's corpus, it's unclear which number controls — the pre-registered one (E2) or R4's AC.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to E2's AC or scope: 'The pre-registered threshold for defect class (4) unnecessary mechanism is >=8/10 detection / <=1/10 FP per R4's AC; the README records this number before any class-4 detector run.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) add to R7's AC that it must emit metrics for all criteria present in criteria_routing.json at the time of each run (dynamic enumeration), or (b) add to R2/R3/R4's ACs a line confirming their criterion ID appears in R7's output before landing.
evidence: Parent SC-B: 'R7 dogfood monitoring: when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
R7 AC: 'emitting per-criterion trailing metrics documented in a README' — generic, doesn't enumerate which criterion IDs
R2/R3/R4 ACs: reference dogfood observation and R7 metrics loosely but don't assert their criterion IDs will appear in R7's output
R4 AC: 'one month of dogfood observation is recorded on this ticket via R7 metrics (catches and FP proxy)'
findings.finding: Parent SC-B requires trailing blocking-FP proxy monitoring with 10%-triggered demotion for ALL landed new criteria. R7 (b1a4) builds the job and documents the alarm rule, but R2 (b080), R3 (3a27), and R4 (03a9) do not explicitly assert that their new criterion IDs will be emitted by R7's instrumentation job — the per-criterion monitoring coverage for the new DET/advisory criteria is assumed but not AC-locked.
findings.location: children b080-fa0b-b1ce-4fad (R2), 3a27-f8fa-c417-43db (R3), 03a9-e6d1-4acc-435d (R4), b1a4-af7a-4a48-465d (R7)
scenarios: R2 lands a new DET criterion. R7's instrumentation job was written before R2's criterion ID existed and doesn't enumerate it. The per-criterion metric for R2's lint never appears in R7's output. FP rate for R2 climbs above 10% undetected.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default permitted dimensions for the 'changed method' re-run (e.g., 'change at least one of: sample size ≥ 20% increase, annotator set, or annotation guide revision') so an executor can act without a round-trip.
evidence: "gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method" — 'changed method' is unspecified
ABSENCE: no list of permitted method-change dimensions (sampling, annotation, prompt, corpus size)
findings.finding: The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE specifies 'one documented re-run with changed method' but does not define what dimension of method change satisfies the re-run requirement (e.g., increased sample size, different annotators, revised annotation guide, changed prompt). An executor encountering an inconclusive result must decide this without guidance, risking inconsistent application across experiments.
findings.location: Context block — 'INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: an experiment that fails its own validity gate... gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method'
scenarios: E1 returns kappa=0.68 (below 0.7); executor must choose a re-run method but has no guidance on what change is sufficient to constitute a valid re-run attempt
Two parallel experiments each apply a different definition of 'changed method,' making cross-experiment precedent inconsistent
findings.suggested_fix: In the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE, append: '(changed method means at least one of: increase sample size by ≥ 20%, replace or add an annotator, or revise the annotation guide — the change and rationale must be recorded in the re-run writeup).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'changed method' in the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE with at least a minimum vocabulary (e.g. 'one or more of: different annotators, ≥50% larger corpus, revised annotation rubric, different inter-rater protocol') so any executor can determine whether a proposed re-run qualifies.
evidence: Plan text: 'gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method; if still not clearly passing, every dependent R-story closes deferred'
ABSENCE: no enum, no examples, no co-located table defines what 'changed method' encompasses or excludes

The E1 experiment (inter-rater kappa >= 0.7) is one example trigger — but whether 'changed method' means different adjudicators, a larger corpus, a refined rubric, or a different annotation interface is unspecified
findings.finding: The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE in the Context section states an inconclusive experiment 'gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method', but 'changed method' is never defined — no value vocabulary, no enum, and no co-located decision table specifies what qualifies as a valid method change (e.g. different annotators, increased sample N, different inter-rater protocol, different prompt). This is a processing protocol referenced without its decision vocabulary.
findings.location: Context — 'INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: an experiment that fails its own validity gate...gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method'
scenarios: An executor designing the re-run for a failed E1 (kappa < 0.7) cannot determine whether simply adding more corpus samples qualifies as 'changed method' or whether a structural change (different raters, different rubric) is required — the gate could be inadvertently bypassed or unnecessarily blocked.
Different child story authors (E2, E3, E5, E6) may interpret 'changed method' differently, producing inconsistent deferral decisions across the experiment set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend R5's child AC) that requires evidence of IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination with the floor-split story before R5 is merged — e.g. a commit message or PR comment confirming the single-bump sequencing was verified.
evidence: 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
No AC references IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION or corpus segmentation coordination
findings.finding: The R5 IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION coordination constraint ('R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly — one bump train, never two half-bumps') is a concrete process deliverable described in Context but has no covering AC. If this coordination fails, corpus segmentation is corrupted, invalidating E5's non-regression gate.
findings.location: Context section, final paragraph ('R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump…')
scenarios: R5 and the floor-split story both bump IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION independently; corpus splits at two different points; E5 non-regression measurement spans a mixed corpus and produces invalid results.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination rule ('one bump train, never two half-bumps' between R5 and the floor-split story) to docs/plan-review-gate.md or CLAUDE.md so it is discoverable from the implementation artifacts after the epic closes.
evidence: The coordination rule is stated inline in the epic description only: 'one bump train, never two half-bumps'
No AC line directs this rule to be written into docs/plan-review-gate.md, CLAUDE.md, or an ADR

If this epic ticket is closed/archived, future contributors working on either R5 or the floor-split story (relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly / large-sleepful-needlefish) have no durable pointer to the constraint
The Success Criteria mandates docs/plan-review-gate.md 'reflects every landed change' but does not explicitly name this coordination contract as something to record there
findings.finding: The IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination rule ('one bump train, never two half-bumps', coordinating R5 with the floor-split story) is a novel cross-component sequencing contract stated only in the epic Context section. No durable design doc (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or docs/plan-review-gate.md update) is directed to capture this rule, risking it being lost when the epic closes.
findings.location: Context section — 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
scenarios: A future contributor picks up R5 or the floor-split story independently and bumps IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION without checking the other story, producing two half-bumps and corrupting the corpus segment boundary
The epic is marked done and the ticket archived; the rule is no longer discoverable from the implementation files
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection to docs/plan-review-gate.md (or the R5 child story's own AC) explicitly recording: 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bumps for R5 and the floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) must be coordinated as a single bump train — claim whichever story is ready first and hold the other until the corpus segment is confirmed clean.' Reference this from both stories' ACs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the epic (or add a one-line scope note immediately after the title) to reflect that recommendations are validated-then-implemented-or-deferred, e.g. 'Validate and implement plan-review decomposition recommendations R1-R7 (eval-gated)'.
evidence: Title: 'Implement plan-review decomposition recommendations R1-R7 from task-decomposition SOTA report'
AC line: 'Each recommendation child (R1-R7) is either landed through Gerrit with its own AC gate terms demonstrably passed … or closed/deferred with the failing result recorded'
INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: 'every dependent R-story closes deferred … ambiguity never rounds up to a pass'
findings.finding: The title promises 'Implement plan-review decomposition recommendations R1-R7' (implying all seven will ship), but the plan body explicitly allows each recommendation to close as deferred if its eval gate fails. A reader following only the title would expect all seven recommendations to be implemented, potentially treating deferral as a failure rather than a designed outcome.
findings.location: Title vs. Acceptance Criteria ('Each recommendation child is either landed ... or closed/deferred with the failing result recorded')
scenarios: A new team member reads the title and later finds R2 or R3 was deferred; they may incorrectly treat the epic as incomplete or block release.
Ticket tracking tools that auto-report 'implemented' vs 'not implemented' based on title may miscount delivered value.
findings.suggested_fix: Change title to: 'Validate and implement plan-review decomposition recommendations R1-R7 (eval-gated)' or prepend the subtitle 'Eval-gated integration of R1-R7' to make the conditional delivery explicit at first glance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the Context or approach section explaining *why* eval-corpus validation (vs production A/B) and direct incorporation with R7 dogfood monitoring are well-fitted for this specific work — not just that they are operator-mandated.
evidence: Context says 'per operator direction (2026-07-15): no A/B testing - validation is eval-based (the E-gates below)' — frames the approach as a constraint, not a positive fit argument.
No section explains WHY eval-based validation is well-suited for this gate at this scale, or why dogfood monitoring is the right post-ship observability form rather than staged rollout.
ABSENCE: the plan has no 'why this approach' paragraph or rationale section, only what-and-how descriptions and cited operator directives.
findings.finding: The plan states the approach (eval-first gate, no A/B, dogfood observation) and cites operator constraints that ruled out alternatives, but never provides a positive rationale for why this approach is well-fitted to the specific problem — e.g. why eval corpora over production A/B are the right validation form for this scale/risk, or why direct incorporation with dogfood monitoring is preferable to a slower flag-based ramp at the size of this codebase.
findings.location: Context section ('Per operator direction (2026-07-15): no A/B testing…') and Success Criteria
scenarios: An implementer picking up a child story that touches criteria_routing.json has no documented rationale for why direct incorporation (no flag) is the right choice for their specific change, making it harder to reason about exceptions or edge cases.
A future reviewer assessing whether to continue this methodology has no stated first-principles argument to evaluate — only precedent and operator direction.
findings.suggested_fix: Add 1–3 sentences to Context, e.g.: 'Eval-corpus validation is preferred over live A/B here because the gate's impact is low-volume and binary (block/advisory), making online significance impractical without months of traffic; the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar already provides sufficient observability for post-ship calibration. Direct incorporation without flags follows the established calibration precedent (calibration-3 demoted T5e via criteria_routing.json without a flag), keeping the registry as the single source of truth.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ordering gate to R7's AC (b1a4) and to R1/R2/R3/R4's ACs: each detection-bearing R-story must confirm R7's instrumentation job is committed before its own Gerrit change lands.
evidence: Epic Context: 'first wave after E1 is R7 + R6 … so dogfood monitoring exists before any detection change lands'
b1a4 AC: no ordering constraint locking R7 before R2/R3/R4
R1/R2/R3/R4 ACs: none gate on R7 instrumentation being committed first
findings.finding: R7's AC does not encode its must-land-before-detection-changes sequencing as a gate condition on R7 itself or on the detection R-stories (R1/R2/R3/R4). The epic Context states 'first wave after E1 is R7 + R6 … so dogfood monitoring exists before any detection change lands,' but b1a4's AC has no ordering constraint, and R1/R2/R3/R4's ACs do not gate on R7 being complete — allowing detection changes to ship without standing monitoring.
findings.location: child b1a4-af7a-4a48-465d (R7) and children 59b7, b080, 3a27, 03a9
scenarios: R2 lands its DET lint before R7's instrumentation job is committed; the blocking-FP proxy for the new criterion is invisible for the first weeks of production; if FP rate spikes above 10% the alarm can't fire.
R4 ships the necessity probe; advisory-application latency data (the primary dogfood signal) is not collected because R7 hasn't run yet.
findings.suggested_fix: In b1a4's AC, add: 'The instrumentation job is committed and re-runnable BEFORE any R2/R3/R4/R1 implementation change lands (prerequisite, verifiable by commit order).' In R1/R2/R3/R4 ACs, add a gate line: 'R7 instrumentation job is committed (verifiable by docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/README.md) before this change lands.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to R5's AC: 'Before claiming, R5 implementer checks the floor-split story's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION outcome and either (a) confirms floor-split bumped and R5 composes into that same version string, or (b) owns the bump if floor-split did not — documented in the commit message; a test asserts only one new version value exists post-landing.'
evidence: Epic Context: 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
R5 AC: 'IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION is bumped in the same change; sidecars persist the new sub-answer losslessly'
No child has an AC owning the multi-story bump reconciliation
findings.finding: IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION coordination between R5 (ea39) and the floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) is stated in the epic Context as 'one bump train, never two half-bumps' but no child owns the reconciliation step — R5's AC says 'bump in the same change' and notes the floor-split 'may itself bump the version, in which case this composes into the same bump train,' but there is no owned artifact or AC in either R5 or the referenced sibling that locks the composition.
findings.location: child ea39-c2d1-e26a-4ee6 (R5)
scenarios: Floor-split story bumps IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION to plan-v3. R5 is claimed separately; its AC says 'bump in the same change' — but if floor-split already bumped, R5 must compose, not re-bump. The 'coordination at claim time' is a procedural note but has no AC or test to enforce it.
Two separate bumps land in separate Gerrit changes; calibration corpus segments split at the wrong boundary, pooling pre- and post-R5 reviews under the same version.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate a single canonical location for the 5886 plan fixture (e.g., docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/fixtures/) and have both R1 and R4 reference the same file; add this to both children's ACs or Scope sections.
evidence: R1 AC: 'every plan in E1's re-verified miss list (expected dc58/db7b/5886) surfaces the decisive finding above the advisory cap' — implies a fixture for 5886
R4 AC: 'the necessity probe fires on the 5886 plan fixture (pinned as a test)'
Neither AC specifies the fixture's file path or format, nor that the two children share one canonical fixture
findings.finding: R4 (03a9) and R1 (59b7) both pin 5886's plan as a test fixture but neither child acknowledges the shared fixture or specifies a canonical location and format, risking divergent pinned representations of the same historical plan in the test suite.
findings.location: children 59b7-69b6-e763-4a83 (R1) and 03a9-e6d1-4acc-435d (R4)
scenarios: R1 commits a 5886 plan fixture under src/rebar/llm/reviewers/fixtures/5886_capability_miss.json. R4 independently commits a 5886 plan fixture under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/fixtures/5886_noop.json. The two fixtures represent the same plan but diverge over time (e.g., one is updated after E1 corrects the miss set). Tests pass against different versions of the same plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a parenthetical in E6's What/AC (and R5's AC) disambiguating 'E4' as a criteria-family label (e.g., 'G6/E4/T3 cohort — where E4 is the criteria-family identifier, distinct from dropped experiment E4').
evidence: E6 What: 'the new sub-answer reads na everywhere outside the G6/E4/T3 cohort'
Epic Context: 'E4 (the in-situ A/B experiment) is deliberately DROPPED per the operator's no-A/B directive'
R5 AC also uses 'G6/E4/T3 cohort' — same ambiguity propagated
findings.finding: E6 (a880) uses 'E4' as a criterion-cohort label ('na everywhere outside the G6/E4/T3 cohort') in a context where 'E4' is also the deliberately dropped experiment identifier. This dual usage is never disambiguated in E6's text, creating a documentation hazard for implementers.
findings.location: child a880-b7e1-dc3e-407c (E6)
scenarios: An implementer reading E6's AC and seeing 'G6/E4/T3 cohort' pauses to determine whether this references the dropped experiment E4 or a rubric criteria family named E4 — the plan never explicitly disambiguates. The implementer may incorrectly scope the cohort or flag it as a contradiction.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'G6/E4/T3 cohort' to 'G6/E4-criterion/T3 cohort (E4 here is the criteria-family label, not the dropped A/B experiment)' in both E6 and R5.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the required artifact for 'R7 dogfood monitoring documented' in AC4 (e.g., 'a schema/config file committed to the repo AND a monitoring section added to docs/plan-review-gate.md describing metric names, collection point, and demotion trigger') so the criterion is unambiguously checkable.
evidence: "with R7 dogfood monitoring documented" — no artifact type, format, or location specified
ABSENCE: no pointer to what R7 monitoring documentation looks like (contrast: the plan precisely locates calibration assets under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/)
findings.finding: AC4 states that 'R7 dogfood monitoring documented' must be satisfied, but does not specify what artifact constitutes 'documented' — a dashboard URL, a schema in code, a section in docs/plan-review-gate.md, or a separate experiment writeup are all plausible but distinct deliverables. An executor cannot determine when this sub-criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'with R7 dogfood monitoring documented'
scenarios: Executor commits a one-line mention in docs/plan-review-gate.md and closes AC4; reviewer expects a structured dashboard or schema definition and rejects it
AC4 stays perpetually open because 'documented' is interpreted differently by submitter and reviewer
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'with R7 dogfood monitoring documented' with 'with R7 dogfood monitoring specified in a committed schema/config and a monitoring section added to docs/plan-review-gate.md (metric names, collection point, 10% demotion trigger documented).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC4) that explicitly verifies the blocking-FP proxy threshold demotion path: e.g. 'R7 instrumentation exposes a per-criterion blocking-FP proxy, and a documented procedure (or automated step) demotes posture to advisory when that proxy exceeds 10%, with at least one test case or runbook entry covering the demotion trigger.'
evidence: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
AC4 says only: 'with R7 dogfood monitoring documented' — no AC verifies the threshold-triggered demotion path exists or fires
findings.finding: The Success Criteria describes an operational demotion mechanism ('when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change') as a behavioral deliverable, but no AC verifies this mechanism is implemented, wired to R7 instrumentation, or testable. AC4 only says 'R7 dogfood monitoring documented' — documentation is not the same as the demotion path being functional.
findings.location: Success Criteria paragraph 2 ('The gate ships with no blocking-FP regression…'); AC line 4
scenarios: R7 ships, monitoring is documented, but the automated or procedural 10% demotion path is never wired up; the success criterion is silently unmet with no AC to catch it.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC4 to read: '…with R7 dogfood monitoring documented AND the blocking-FP proxy demotion path verified: a runbook entry or automated gate confirms that a criterion whose trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10% is demoted to advisory posture in criteria_routing.json.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (e.g., Gerrit revert procedure, emergency criteria_routing.json revert commit, or IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION rollback steps) for landed changes to criteria_routing.json and the packaged rubrics, given config-flag rollback is explicitly prohibited.
evidence: 'no config-flag or overlay gating - operator direction 2026-07-15'
The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE covers only experiment-phase ambiguity, not post-ship rollback
No Gerrit revert procedure, back-out commit strategy, or rollback sequence is named anywhere in the plan
'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time' — coordination described but no remediation if bump causes corpus regression post-ship
findings.finding: The plan explicitly forbids config-flag or overlay gating (operator direction 2026-07-15) and describes no alternative rollback/back-out mechanism for landed changes to criteria_routing.json, packaged rubrics, or IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION if a shipped change degrades production. The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE handles pre-ship experiment failures but is silent on post-ship regression remediation.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'validated changes are incorporated DIRECTLY into the packaged review process (no config-flag or overlay gating)'; Context: INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE
scenarios: A landed R2/R3/R4 routing change causes an unexpected blocking-FP spike beyond the 10% threshold before R7 dogfood monitoring catches it
R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump segments the corpus incorrectly and requires reverting — no procedure stated
A criteria change ships and a downstream consumer (e.g. CI gate) breaks — no back-out path articulated
findings.suggested_fix: In the Success Criteria or a dedicated 'Rollback' section, state: 'If a landed change triggers a blocking-FP proxy > 10% or a production regression, the remediation path is: (1) immediate Gerrit revert of the offending commit, (2) R7 dogfood metrics serve as the trip wire, (3) for IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bumps, a follow-on bump to a new version restoring prior behavior is the rollback unit.' This preserves the no-flag constraint while providing an explicit reversibility mechanism.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name an explicit documentation artifact (e.g., update to code-review's own docs or a shared review_kernel README/ADR) that R5's child story must deliver when modifying the shared review_kernel and bumping IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION, so code-review consumers are not left with stale references.
evidence: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'
The plan's doc obligations name only docs/experiments/ writeups and docs/plan-review-gate.md — no code-review-side documentation update is mentioned
The IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination rule is described in Context prose but no ADR or module-level contract doc is named for the shared kernel versioning policy
findings.finding: R5 modifies src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ which is explicitly described as SHARED with code-review, but the plan names no documentation update for code-review consumers of the shared module when R5 lands its changes and IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump.
findings.location: Context: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'
scenarios: Code-review consumers of review_kernel have stale docs after R5's interface or version changes land
The shared kernel versioning policy (one bump train, never two half-bumps) is an undocumented architectural decision with no ADR, leaving future maintainers without rationale
findings.suggested_fix: Add to R5's implementation targets: 'update docs/review-kernel.md (or equivalent) to reflect the version bump policy and any interface changes; coordinate with code-review doc owner if a separate doc exists.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to E3's AC or Scope: 'E1's outcome_corpus.jsonl must be committed (with kappa >= 0.7) before E3's sample is drawn; E3 is sequenced after E1.'
evidence: E3 AC: 'The stratified ~200-review sample is committed (ids + strata) before the replay runs, drawn from the E1 outcome corpus'
E3 Scope: no mention of E1 as a sequencing dependency
E1 AC: kappa.py exits non-zero if kappa < 0.7, labels declared unusable
findings.finding: E3 does not declare E1 as an explicit prerequisite even though its sample is drawn from the E1 outcome corpus. If E3 begins before E1's outcome_corpus.jsonl is committed (or before E1's kappa validity gate passes), E3's stratified sample has no grounded source.
findings.location: child def5-e68d-6c2f-413b (E3)
scenarios: E3 begins ticket sampling before E1 is complete (e.g., working in parallel); the 'E1 outcome corpus' doesn't exist yet, so E3 must construct its own sample using a different method — introducing corpus divergence that undermines E3's comparability with R1's gate row.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add E2 as an explicit prerequisite to R4's Scope and ACs: 'Gated on E2 (unnecessary-mechanism mutants corpus built and labeled); sequenced after E2 completion.'
evidence: R4 AC: 'the necessity probe fires on the 5886 plan fixture (pinned as a test) and on >=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants with <=1/10 controls flagged'
R4 Scope: 'Sequenced AFTER the floor-split story' — no mention of E2
R1 What: 'gated on E2+E3'; R3 What: 'gated on E2+E6'
findings.finding: R4 (03a9) does not list E2 as an explicit dependency in its Scope or ACs, unlike R1 ('gated on E2+E3') and R3 ('gated on E2+E6'). R4's retrospective uses E2 defect-class mutants but could be attempted before E2's corpus exists.
findings.location: child 03a9-e6d1-4acc-435d (R4)
scenarios: R4 is picked up after the floor-split but before E2's mutation corpus is built; the implementer reads R4's AC and concludes that the 5886 fixture alone suffices (it's pinned), proceeding to land without the E2 mutant validation — since E2 dependency is unstated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to E2, E3, E6, and R7's ACs: 'If E1's kappa validity gate fails (kappa < 0.7), this story closes deferred with that result recorded (per epic INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE).'
evidence: E1 AC: 'if kappa < 0.7 the labels are declared unusable, kappa.py exits non-zero'
Epic Context INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: 'every dependent R-story closes deferred with the results recorded'
E2/E3/E6/R7: no AC line for 'if E1 validity gate fails, this story closes deferred'
findings.finding: E1's kappa validity gate failure cascades to E2, E3, E6, and R7 — all of which consume E1 corpora — but none of those children has an AC that closes them deferred on E1 validity failure. The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE's cascade is stated at the epic level but not wired into the downstream children.
scenarios: E1 runs, kappa = 0.65 (below 0.7), labels declared unusable. E2 implementer reads E2's ACs — nothing says to close deferred; they attempt to build mutants anyway using the unreliable corpus. E3 proceeds similarly. The cascade failure is only documented at the epic level, which may not be consulted during child execution.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
